feedback.text: With video games moving more and more into being sold digitally, I think its extremely important for them to be protected under this act so that people can continue to use the products that they buy long after they stop being the in thing to buy. Publishers shouldn't be allowed to add an internet requirement to old games only to delete that same service so that you buy the next shiny thing. People can buy an audio book 5/6 years ago for example and read it today without needing to worry so so should video games. Also any chance you could add a ruling that prevents companies like Visa and Mastercard to refuse to accept payments for products some right wing group like Collective Shout disagree's with because it doesn't suit their weird world view?
